commit.message:
feat: add WebSocket real-time messaging

Replace 4-second polling with a persistent WebSocket connection:
- Add ConnectionManager (per-user broadcast to all open tabs/devices)
- Add /ws endpoint with session-cookie auth
- Broadcast {type: new_message} on send, send-image, send-file
- Broadcast {type: clipboard_update} on clipboard POST
- Frontend connects on load with exponential backoff reconnect (1s→30s)
- 25s keepalive ping prevents idle disconnects on mobile browsers
- 4s poll kept as automatic fallback when WS is not connected

commit.message:
fix(bundles): use full git+https#subdirectory= URIs for behavior self-references

Relative paths and namespace:path both fail when loaded via git+https#subdirectory=
because Foundation resolves the subpath relative to repo root, not bundle directory.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Evaluation] Fix red team status tracking, cache key mismatch, and evaluation error handling (#45517)

* Fix red team status tracking, cache key mismatch, and evaluation error handling

Bug 1 - Status tracking: _determine_run_status now treats 'pending' and
'running' entries as 'failed' instead of 'in_progress'. By the time this
method runs the scan is finished, so leftover 'pending' entries (from
skipped risk categories or Foundry execution failures) indicate failure,
not ongoing work.

Bug 2 - Cache key mismatch: _execute_attacks_with_foundry now uses
get_attack_objective_from_risk_category() to build the cache lookup key,
matching the caching logic in _get_attack_objectives. Previously,
ungrounded_attributes objectives were cached under 'isa' but looked up
under 'ungrounded_attributes', causing them to be silently skipped.

Bug 3 - Evaluation error handling: RAIServiceScorer now detects when the
RAI evaluation service returns an error response (properties.outcome ==
'error', e.g. ServiceInvocationException) and raises RuntimeError. This
causes PyRIT to treat the score as UNDETERMINED instead of using the
erroneous passed=False to incorrectly mark the attack as successful,
which was inflating ASR.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add changelog entries for status tracking, cache key, and scoring fixes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Standard Task Workflow ceremony (#102)

Documents the end-to-end workflow for task execution:
- Phase 1: Setup (branch creation, sync with main)
- Phase 2: Planning (analyze, document, get approval)
- Phase 3: Implementation (work, test, commit with pre-push gates)
- Phase 4: Review & Merge (PR, CI checks, merge)
- Phase 5: Cleanup (return to main, sync)

Integrates with existing ceremonies:
- Build Repair Check (pre-push hook)
- Code Review (PR trigger)
- Merged-PR Branch Guard (decision)

feat: Homebrew tap auto-update on release + brew install instructions (#313)

## Changes

- **build.yml**: Adds two steps to `create-release` job:
  - `Compute Mac SHA256` — hashes `PolyPilot.zip` for the cask
- `Update Homebrew tap` — dispatches `release-published` event to
[PureWeen/homebrew-tap](https://github.com/PureWeen/homebrew-tap) with
version + SHA256 (skipped for prereleases)
  
- **README.md**: Adds `Install with Homebrew` section (`brew tap
PureWeen/tap && brew install --cask polypilot`) and fixes the Copilot
CLI install command

## Homebrew Tap

The tap at
[PureWeen/homebrew-tap](https://github.com/PureWeen/homebrew-tap) is
already set up with:
- `brew install --cask polypilot` — Mac Catalyst GUI app (auto-installs
`copilot-cli` as a dependency)
- `brew install polypilot-console` — Cross-platform CLI
- Auto-update workflow triggered by this PR's dispatch step

## Setup Required

Add a `HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN` secret to this repo — a fine-grained PAT
scoped to `PureWeen/homebrew-tap` with **Contents: Read and write**
permission.

commit.message:
fix(stealth): remove --enable-automation flag in stealth mode

When hidden=true (stealth mode), skip --enable-automation from Chrome's
DEFAULT_ARGS. This flag was causing:
1. The "Chrome is being controlled by automated test software" infobar
2. navigator.webdriver = true at the browser process level

Now filtered out in BrowserConfig::launch() when stealth is active.
Combined with the CDP-level patches (inject_persistent_stealth), this
provides complete stealth coverage at both process and protocol level.

Verified in both headless and headed daemon modes:
- No automation infobar
- navigator.webdriver = false
- navigator.plugins.length = 5
- UA clean (no HeadlessChrome)
- "Persistent stealth patches registered for all pages" on session start

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add tiny SDPO training experiment report and logs (#5)

* Add tiny SDPO training experiment report and logs

Run 2-step overfit experiment on Qwen3-4B with LCBv6 (4 train tasks,
10 eval tasks). Teacher KL decreased 27.5% across steps, confirming
distillation signal. Zero accuracy expected at this scale. Includes
cost estimate (~$0.03 API equivalent) and comparison with SDPO paper.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Add token accounting and grade timing metrics

- env.py: Add grade_time_s metric to SDPOCodeEnv.step() to separate
  sandbox grading time from inference time in future runs
- train_on_policy.py: Log teacher_kl_prefill_tokens (tokens sent for
  teacher logprob forward passes), train_total_tokens, and
  train_action_tokens (masked tokens the optimizer processes)
- experiment_report.md: Fix cost estimate to use actual Tinker API
  pricing instead of Alibaba Cloud rates; add prefill/sample/train
  breakdown; note timing granularity limitations

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Re-run experiment with token/timing metrics, rewrite report

New metrics now visible in logs:
- grade_time_s: sandbox grading is only 1.5% of wall time
- teacher_kl_prefill_tokens: exact teacher logprob token counts
- train_total_tokens / train_action_tokens: optimizer token counts

Report rewritten with actual Tinker API pricing ($0.07/1M prefill,
$0.22/1M sample, $0.22/1M train). Total cost: ~2.4 cents.
Inference dominates at 90% of wall time.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Use timed() context manager for grading timing in env.step()

Replace manual time.monotonic() bookkeeping with the timed() context
manager from tinker_cookbook. The metric key changes from "grade_time_s"
to "time/grade", consistent with all other timing metrics in wandb
(time/sample, time/train, etc.). Remove unused `import time`.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Add run log for v3 experiment (with timed() grading)

Re-ran the tiny overfit experiment after switching to timed("grade", metrics).
Confirms time/grade metric flows correctly through wandb logging.
Results: 8.4 min wall-clock, ~2.6 cents, 0% accuracy (expected).

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Add 10x extended SDPO experiment (9 steps, teacher KL ↓74%)

Ran a 20-step experiment (9 completed before timeout) with the same
config as the 2-step baseline. Key finding: teacher KL decreased
consistently from 0.019 to 0.005 over 9 steps, confirming SDPO
training signal works. Accuracy remains 0% because LCBv6 is too
hard for a 4B model with 4096 max tokens.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Add raw per-run log files from 10x experiment

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Update report with 7 repeated runs + 9-step extended data

Combined analysis of 7 independent 2-step runs (variance estimates)
and 1 extended 9-step run (learning curve). Teacher KL decreased in
6/6 repeated runs (mean -34%) and 74% over 9 steps. Zero accuracy
persists due to task difficulty, not training failure.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Update remaining log files with complete output

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Final log file sync

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Update run_20step.log with final output

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

Co-authored-by: bzz <5582506+bzz@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 10 CUDA vs Triton side-by-side comparison scripts

Each script JIT-compiles a CUDA kernel and runs the equivalent Triton kernel
on the EXACT same input tensor, then verifies numerical match.

Basic (4):
  01 Vector Add — exact match (diff=0), 10M float32
  02 Reduction — sum 16M floats, rtol=1e-4
  03 MatMul — 1024x1024, atol=1e-2
  04 Histogram — 256 bins, exact integer match

Advanced (3):
  05 Coalescing — 240 GB/s coalesced vs 55 GB/s strided (both frameworks)
  06 Tensor Cores — WMMA vs tl.dot fp16 matmul, max diff 0.0005
  07 Softmax — row-wise online softmax, max diff 7.5e-9

Production (3):
  08 Fusion — scale+bias+ReLU fused, atol=1e-6
  09 Quantization — per-row INT8 quant/dequant, exact int8 match
  10 LayerNorm — (2048,768) vs torch F.layer_norm, atol=1e-5

Includes run_all.py to execute all comparisons and print summary table.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

which is handled by Azure/functions-action@v1. Replaces manual func CLI

azure-functions-core-tools install step. Uses az functionapp list for
reliable app name discovery.

commit.message:
Sprint 37: DirectMsg sync dispatch, runInline fixes, runInlineResult

- Add DirectMsg of 'msg to Cmd DU for truly synchronous dispatch
  (processed in same drain loop iteration before render, unlike Delay(0))
- Cmd.ofMsg now uses DirectMsg (was Delay(0,msg), semantically incorrect)
- Add Cmd.nextFrame = Delay(0, msg) for honest 'next frame' naming
- Fix runInlineWith resize: reallocate both buffers on terminal resize
- Add Console.CancelKeyPress handler to runInlineWith for clean Ctrl+C exit
- Add cleanup() DRY local for shared exit path in runInlineWith
- Add App.runInlineResult: returns typed 'result option via resultFn projection
- Update Testing.fs processCmd to handle DirectMsg (same as Delay(0))
- Update test: Cmd.map transforms message type -> expects DirectMsg not Delay
- 11 new Sprint 37 TDD tests (all passing)
- Total: 1,794 tests, 0 failures

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix voice button crash: add missing privacy permissions and mic authorization

The app crashed with TCC __TCC_CRASHING_DUE_TO_PRIVACY_VIOLATION__ when
tapping the mic button because:

1. NSMicrophoneUsageDescription and NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription
   were in the source Info.plist but not merged into the built app plist
   on Mac Catalyst. Fixed by adding a PartialAppManifest in the csproj.

2. Missing com.apple.security.personal-information.speech-recognition
   sandbox entitlement for speech recognition TCC access.

3. AppleSpeechService never requested explicit microphone authorization
   before starting AVAudioEngine. Added AVCaptureDevice.RequestAccess
   and AVAudioSession configuration before engine startup.

- src/database/connection.py delega en sanri-sdk (Database, get_connection)
- src/database/repository.py reescrito con get_connection() + RealDictCursor
  y Database.execute() con psycopg2.extras.Json para columnas JSONB
- Llamadas a BD envueltas con asyncio.to_thread() para no bloquear el event loop
- src/config.py elimina campos DSN de BD (SDK los gestiona vía Vault)
- main.py elimina gestión del pool (init/close)
- requirements.txt: asyncpg → sanri-sdk[postgres]
- .env.example muestra VAULT_ADDR/VAULT_TOKEN en lugar de DSN

refactor: apply 7 code quality improvements across BoardGame project

- Fix 1: Create ITemplateReader interface in SharedKernel for cross-module SQL template reads, with TemplateReader implementation in Infrastructure using OutboxDbContext. Update Live GenerateImage handler to use ITemplateReader instead of raw SQL. Remove TemplateReadModel from CrossModuleReadModels.

- Fix 2: Create UploadImageCommandValidator with FluentValidation for file size and MIME type validation. Remove inline file validation from LiveImagesController (except null check needed before command construction).

- Fix 3: Unify CommandResult into SharedKernel with ToActionResult() method. Delete 4 duplicate CommandResult files from Characters, Gallery, Identity, and Sessions modules. Replace all 19 inline Live result types (CreateLiveSessionResult, JoinSessionResult, etc.) with the unified CommandResult. Update all handlers and queries across all modules to use BoardGame.Shared.Kernel.CQRS.CommandResult.

- Fix 4: Convert all controllers to primary constructors. Applies to LiveSessionsController, LiveImagesController, LiveChatController, SoundboardController, CinemaController, CharactersController, CharacterSheetsController, GalleryController, ImageGenerationController, TemplatesController, SessionImagesController, and SpotifyController.

- Fix 5: Extract magic strings into SharedKernel constants. Create LiveSessionStatus (Active/Ended) and ParticipantRole (Master/Player) constants. Replace all occurrences in Live handlers.

- Fix 6: Extract SignalR event strings into HubEvents constants class. Replace all string literals in Live handlers, consumers, and Characters sheet handlers with constants like HubEvents.SessionEnded, HubEvents.ParticipantJoined, HubEvents.ImageNew, etc.

- Fix 7: Add CancellationToken parameter to all Live controller action methods and pass to sender.Send(). Also applied to Characters, Gallery, and Sessions controllers for consistency.

commit.message:
fix(critical): stealth-by-default on ALL browser sessions

BREAKING FIX: Every browser session now automatically applies the full
stealth stack regardless of how it was created:

1. Chrome-level: --disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled (.hide())
   added to ALL BrowserSession::launch_* methods (headless AND headed)

2. CDP-level: inject_persistent_stealth() called in daemon's
   get_or_create_session() — patches navigator.webdriver, plugins,
   chrome.runtime, visibilityState, UA, languages, WebGL on every new
   session including pre-warmed pool sessions

Verified stealth in headless mode:
- navigator.webdriver = false ✓
- navigator.plugins.length = 5 ✓
- window.chrome.runtime exists ✓
- document.hidden = false ✓
- UA clean (no HeadlessChrome) ✓
- navigator.languages = realistic ✓

commit.message:
Reformat animals.py to canonical style

- All imports at top; removed cv2, PIL, unused prints
- DATASET_MODE constant; EXPERIMENT_NAME = f'animal_{DATASET_MODE}'
- NUM_CLASSES=3 with trimap comment
- TO_REMOVE converted to set for O(1) lookup
- Stratified split preserved with labels_all / labels_tv
- ALL_CAPS constants, # ==== section headers, for cfg loop, best_model

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(orchestrator): split run_orchestrator into composable phases

Extract the monolithic run_orchestrator function (~300 lines, C901)
into composable phases with a shared _OrchestratorContext dataclass:

- _setup_orchestrator(): agent identity, logging, signal handlers,
  beads backfill/recovery, beta tester, and config resolution
- _run_preflight(): delegates to shared handle_preflight_checks()
  from parallel_support, eliminating ~60 lines of duplicated
  preflight health check logic between orchestrator.py and parallel.py
- _run_main_loop(): sequential work-selection and dispatch loop
- _finalize_session(): unchanged (already extracted)

The _OrchestratorContext.finalize() method reduces boilerplate at
each exit point in the sequential loop.

Also adds optional cfg parameter to handle_preflight_checks() so
callers can pass their own config object instead of relying on
get_config().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: apply valid PR #162 Copilot inline review suggestions

- api-client.ts: redirect to /auth/login unconditionally after refresh
  failure (was skipping tRPC calls, leaving app in logged-out state)
- api-client.ts: fix trpcMutation to POST raw input body (was wrapping
  in {json:...}; backend expects plain JSON object)
- api-client.ts: update wire-format doc comment to match actual backend
- authService.ts: document that role is server-scoped; User.role is a
  placeholder default until per-server role fetching is added
- AuthContext.tsx: isAdmin() accepts optional serverOwnerId param; when
  provided, checks user.id === serverOwnerId (reliable ownership check)
- ServerSettingsPage: use isAdmin(server.ownerId) so owners can access
  their own server settings
- ChannelSettingsPage: add serverOwnerId prop; pass through to isAdmin()
- channel settings route: parallel-fetch server to supply serverOwnerId

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[docs] Reduce bloat in getting-started/k3s.mdx (#2932)

* docs: reduce bloat in getting-started/k3s.mdx

- Merge redundant 'What is K3s' bullet list and 'When to Use K3s'
  sub-section into concise prose (saves ~15 lines)
- Remove verbose 'KSail will:' explanation from Step 2 (saves ~7 lines)
- Replace large ASCII architecture diagram with a single prose paragraph
  (saves ~25 lines)
- Replace full Distribution Comparison table with link to Support Matrix
  per page ownership map (saves ~16 lines)
- Condense troubleshooting items: remove 'Solutions:' headers and verbose
  comments; item 5 uses prose instead of a code block (saves ~30 lines)
- Merge triple-sectioned Next Steps into a single flat list (saves ~10 lines)

Total: 410 to 297 lines (27.6% reduction)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
